candidates.proposedTitle: Stop Letting AI Choose Your Video Strategy
candidates.idea: A practical framework for using AI as a research assistant while keeping creator judgment in control.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Creator at desk rejecting a chaotic AI suggestion cloud and choosing one clear package card.
candidates.viewerPromise: Learn how to use AI for faster video ideation without outsourcing taste, positioning, or final judgment.
candidates.targetViewer: Serious solo creator experimenting with AI tools but worried about generic content.
candidates.mainRisk: Could become abstract unless grounded in a real example workflow.
shortsIdeas: The 90/10 rule for AI video ideas
Why AI should not pick your final title
One question that exposes generic AI ideas
How to veto an AI package
candidates.why_this_matters_now: Creators are rapidly adopting AI ideation tools and need a sane way to use them without flattening their voice.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: The principle of separating research speed from editorial judgment remains useful across tool cycles.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It matches VIDTOOLZ as practical video creation in the AI era with critical tester instincts.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can show the real workflow, the veto points, and the tradeoffs instead of selling AI hype.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Solo creators are searching for AI workflows but still need confidence that the output will not feel generic.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Use a screen-recorded comparison of raw AI suggestions, a structured package scorecard, and a final human-selected package.

candidates.proposedTitle: My AI Video Idea Filter for Serious Creators
candidates.idea: Show a scoring filter that turns a list of rough AI-assisted topics into one practical video package.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Ten rough ideas entering a funnel, one clean YouTube package card leaving.
candidates.viewerPromise: Get a repeatable way to compare ideas before wasting time scripting or recording.
candidates.targetViewer: Solo creator with too many AI-generated ideas and no clear selection system.
candidates.mainRisk: The scoring framework must feel useful, not arbitrary.
shortsIdeas: Score your video idea before scripting
The fastest way to reject weak AI ideas
Why one clear promise beats ten topics
A better use for ChatGPT video ideas
candidates.why_this_matters_now: AI can generate far more ideas than creators can produce, so selection discipline is becoming the bottleneck.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: Creators always need prioritization, even as AI research tools change.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It turns messy creator workflow into a compact operational tool.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can demonstrate the actual decision process instead of only listing prompts.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Creators want less ideation noise and more confidence choosing the next video.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Build the package cards on screen, sort by score, reject weak ideas, then explain why the winner is strongest.

candidates.proposedTitle: Build a YouTube Package Before You Write the Script
candidates.idea: Explain why title, thumbnail, promise, risk, and Shorts angles should be chosen before writing a full script.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Script document blocked by a missing package checklist.
candidates.viewerPromise: Avoid weak scripts by locking the viewer-facing package before drafting.
candidates.targetViewer: Creator who starts scripting too early and loses the core promise.
candidates.mainRisk: Could overlap with generic YouTube advice unless tied to a concrete package workflow.
candidates.why_this_matters_now: AI makes it easy to generate scripts quickly, which increases the risk of scripting weak ideas.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: Packaging before production is a durable YouTube workflow principle.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It links AI speed with production discipline.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can show the package as an operational artifact, not vague advice.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Creators frequently struggle with videos that are technically complete but poorly packaged.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Use a before/after package card and show how the script outline changes after the package is clarified.

candidates.proposedTitle: The Creator Workflow AI Cannot Replace
candidates.idea: A positioning video about the parts of solo creator work that AI can accelerate versus the parts the creator must own.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: AI robot handling research while creator holds the final decision stamp.
candidates.viewerPromise: Understand where AI helps and where it quietly weakens your creative edge.
candidates.targetViewer: Creator anxious about AI replacing their workflow or voice.
candidates.mainRisk: May become philosophical if not grounded in examples.
shortsIdeas: The creator job that still matters
candidates.why_this_matters_now: Creators are actively reassessing their role as AI tools enter every production stage.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: The human judgment versus automation question will continue across tool generations.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It supports the practical, non-hype VIDTOOLZ stance.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can frame it through actual package and production decisions rather than ideology.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: AI anxiety and workflow uncertainty are strong creator pain points.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Use a simple split workflow diagram and walk through which tasks AI handles versus which require creator judgment.

candidates.proposedTitle: I Tested an AI YouTube Idea Workflow
candidates.idea: Run a real test of AI-assisted idea discovery, package generation, and final human selection.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Scoreboard comparing AI-generated packages with a selected winner.
candidates.viewerPromise: See what AI can and cannot do when asked to build YouTube package candidates.
candidates.targetViewer: Creator curious about AI ideation workflows and skeptical of perfect demos.
candidates.mainRisk: Requires a convincing real test and enough screen capture to be credible.
shortsIdeas: The best AI package surprised me
candidates.why_this_matters_now: Tool testing content has immediate relevance while creators evaluate AI workflows.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: The testing method can remain useful even when specific tools change.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It matches the critical tester side of VIDTOOLZ.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can judge tools by production usefulness instead of novelty.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Creators want grounded AI tool tests that show real limitations.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Record the full candidate generation workflow, then cut it down into a scored comparison and final selection.

candidates.proposedTitle: The 10-Card System for Choosing Your Next Video
candidates.idea: Introduce a simple review surface where 10 candidate packages compete before one is selected.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Ten cards on a board with one highlighted winner.
candidates.viewerPromise: Use a compact card system to compare ideas, risks, titles, thumbnails, and Shorts potential.
candidates.targetViewer: Creator who wants a more visual way to choose between content ideas.
candidates.mainRisk: May feel too meta unless linked to a real production decision.
candidates.why_this_matters_now: Creators have more idea inputs than ever and need fast comparison surfaces.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: Visual comparison remains useful regardless of where ideas come from.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It turns a creator decision into a practical local tool.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can show both the tool and the production thinking behind it.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Many creators prefer visual workflows over long prompt outputs.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Show the local card dashboard and narrate the selection criteria while choosing one winner.

candidates.proposedTitle: Your AI Video Ideas Need a Kill Switch
candidates.idea: Make the case for rejecting weak AI-generated packages quickly before they consume production energy.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Large red reject stamp over generic AI video ideas.
candidates.viewerPromise: Learn the rejection criteria that keep your channel from filling with generic AI-shaped videos.
candidates.targetViewer: Creator overwhelmed by AI suggestions and afraid to discard ideas.
candidates.mainRisk: Negative framing may be less broadly appealing than a positive selection workflow.
shortsIdeas: This AI idea is a trap
One reason to kill a video idea
candidates.why_this_matters_now: AI has reduced the cost of ideation, making bad ideas cheaper and more numerous.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: Rejecting weak ideas is a permanent creator skill.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: It reinforces practical judgment over hype.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ can give concrete rejection criteria instead of vague warnings.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Creators often need permission and criteria to discard ideas.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Use three weak sample packages and show exactly why each gets rejected.

candidates.proposedTitle: The Future of AI Video Creation
candidates.idea: A broad trend video about where AI video tools are heading.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Futuristic AI interface with video icons.
candidates.viewerPromise: Understand what AI might mean for video creation.
candidates.targetViewer: General creator interested in AI trends.
candidates.mainRisk: Too broad, speculative, and not distinct enough for VIDTOOLZ.
candidates.why_this_matters_now: AI video trend coverage is timely.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: The broad framing may age quickly as tools change.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: Weak fit because it is less practical and too general.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ would need to narrow it into a real workflow test.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Trend interest exists but competition is high and intent is unfocused.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Reject for v1; if revived, convert into a specific tool workflow test.

candidates.idea: A listicle covering popular AI tools for research, scripting, thumbnails, editing, and publishing.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Grid of tool logos with ranking numbers.
candidates.viewerPromise: Find the best AI tools for YouTube creators.
candidates.mainRisk: High maintenance, crowded topic, and likely to become outdated quickly.
shortsIdeas: Do not use every AI tool
candidates.why_this_matters_now: Creators are searching for AI tool recommendations.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: Weak long-term relevance because tool rankings change quickly.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: Poor v1 fit because it encourages broad tool coverage over production principles.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ would need hands-on testing criteria, which is too much for this first package UI test.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Search demand may exist, but competition and update burden are high.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Reject for now; later split into individual practical tool tests.

candidates.proposedTitle: AI Will Change Everything for Creators
candidates.idea: A broad opinion piece about AI disruption in creator work.
candidates.thumbnailConcept: Creator silhouette facing giant AI wave.
candidates.viewerPromise: Understand why AI will transform creator work.
candidates.targetViewer: Broad creator audience interested in AI disruption.
candidates.mainRisk: Too hype-driven and not specific enough to the VIDTOOLZ practical promise.
candidates.why_this_stays_relevant: Broad prediction videos age poorly without specific practical value.
candidates.why_this_fits_vidtoolz: Weak fit because it leans toward hype instead of grounded workflow.
candidates.why_vidtoolz_can_make_it_better: VIDTOOLZ could only improve it by narrowing into a concrete creator task.
candidates.audience_demand_rationale: Broad curiosity exists, but audience intent is less actionable.
candidates.suggested_production_approach: Reject for v1; use its concerns as context for more practical package candidates.
